title: Generating Customized XML
text: Generating Customized XML To generate XML data, you can use the operations available from the UXMLWRITER component API. To generate an XML document: For each XML document you want to generate, create an instance of the UXMLWRITER component. For example, to generate three XML documents, create three instances. variables handle uxml1 handle uxml2 handle uxml3 endvariables newinstance "UXMLWRITER", uxml1 newinstance "UXMLWRITER", uxml2 newinstance "UXMLWRITER", uxml3 Copy Call the STARTDOCUMENT operation for each instance. If you want the document to contain a header ( <xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"> ), set the boolean parameter of this operation to true. For example, the following will generate the header for instance uxml1 and uxml2, but not for uxml3: uxml1->startdocument("true") uxml2->startdocument(1) uxml3->startdocument("false") Copy Add XML elements, attributes and comments using the operations available on the UXMLWRITER component. Retrieve the resulting XML documents with the GETDOCUMENT operation. For example, the following retrieves the resulting XML documents into a non-database field or a global variable. uxml1->getdocument(DUMMY, "true") ; document 1 < 10240 bytes uxml2->getdocument($10, "true") ; document 2 < 10240 bytes ; Document 3 is more than 10240 bytes: $1 = uxml3->getdocument(FIELD1, "true") while ($1 > 0) $1 = uxml3->getdocument($2, "true") FIELD1 = "%%FIELD1%%%%%$1%%%" endwhile Copy Note: GETDOCUMENT can only retrieve a maximum of 10240 bytes, so you may need to issue multiple calls to this operation. This example assumes that documents 1 and 2 are known to be always smaller than 10240 bytes in size. Manipulate the data as required. For example, create an XML file by using filedump , or store the XML into the database. Delete the instances of UXMLWRITER when they are no longer required. The memory occupied by XML documents that are being generated is not freed until the instances are deleted. To free this memory and prevent a memory leak, it is very important to delete any unused component instances. For example, the following deletes the three XML instances, and frees the memory occupied by the three XML documents. deleteinstance uxml1 deleteinstance uxml2 deleteinstance uxml3 Copy Related Links UXMLWRITER UXMLWRITER is an internal Uniface component that enables you to generate XML documents rather than having to code each character in the XML stream yourself.
